## Validate **init** Notebooks

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a

### validate_inits

>  validate_inits (project:Optional[str]=None, fix:bool=False)

\*Validate that every 00\_\_init\_\_ notebook has correct default_exp.\*

Accepts numbered-directory prefixes and leaf-only module paths for
organizational notebook layouts.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>fix</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>If True, automatically fix incorrect default_exp values.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result with ‘problems’ list and ‘fixed’ count.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## Lint Rules

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/tools/lint.py#L165"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### lint_rules

>  lint_rules (project:Optional[str]=None, fix_relative:bool=False)

*Lint notebooks for nbdev conventions.*

Checks for: - Manual **all** definitions (not allowed) - Relative
imports (should be absolute) - README.md being generated - Duplicate
exports across notebooks

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>fix_relative</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>If True, convert relative imports to absolute.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result with ‘issues’ list and ‘modified’
count.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/tools/lint.py#L304"

### lint_imports

>  lint_imports (project:Optional[str]=None,
>                    ignore_patterns:Optional[List[str]]=None)

*Check for unused imports in notebook cells.*

Analyzes each code cell to find imports that are never used within that
cell. Uses AST parsing for accurate detection.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias. Uses current project if not specified.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ignore_patterns</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>List of import names to ignore (e.g., [‘typing’,
‘annotations’]).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result with ‘issues’ list of unused imports per
cell.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/tools/lint.py#L476"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### lint_types

>  lint_types (project:Optional[str]=None, strict:bool=False)

*Check type annotations in notebook cells.*

Validates that exported functions and classes have type hints. Uses AST
parsing to detect missing annotations.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias. Uses current project if not specified.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>strict</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>If True, require all parameters to have type hints.<br>If False
(default), only check return type annotations.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result with ‘issues’ list of missing type
annotations.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/tools/lint.py#L576"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### auto_fix_lint

>  auto_fix_lint (project:Optional[str]=None, fix_relative:bool=True,
>                     fix_inits:bool=True, dry_run:bool=False)

*Auto-fix common lint issues in notebooks.*

Automatically fixes: - Relative imports -\> absolute imports - Incorrect
default_exp in 00\_\_init\_\_.ipynb notebooks

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias. Uses current project if not specified.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>fix_relative</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>If True, convert relative imports to absolute.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>fix_inits</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>If True, fix incorrect default_exp in <strong>init</strong>
notebooks.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>dry_run</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>If True, report what would be fixed without making changes.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result with ‘fixes’ list of applied/proposed
fixes.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## [`lint_notebook_structure`](https://s01st.github.io/nbdev-mcp/11_tools/lint.html#lint_notebook_structure)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a

target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### lint_notebook_structure

>  lint_notebook_structure (project:Optional[str]=None, fix:bool=False)

*Check and fix notebook structure for nbdev conventions.*

Checks that notebooks have: - Standard ending structure (## Next, empty
export, \## Export, nbdev_export) - Properly formatted headers with
backticks for code names

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias. Uses current project if not specified.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>fix</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>If True, automatically fix structure issues.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result dict with:<br>- ok: bool - Success status<br>-
issues: List[Dict] - Structure issues found<br>- fixed: int - Number of
notebooks fixed (if fix=True)<br>- pretty: str - Formatted
output</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### lint_private_attributes

>  lint_private_attributes (project:Optional[str]=None,
>                               allow_patterns:Optional[List[str]]=None)

*Flag private attributes (self.\_xxx) in exported classes.*

MCP tools should be stateless. Private attributes suggest hidden state
that persists between calls, which breaks the MCP model.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>allow_patterns</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>List of attribute name patterns to allow (e.g., [’_cache’,
’_lock’]).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result with ‘issues’ list of private attribute
usages.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/tools/lint.py#L831"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### lint_cell_complexity

>  lint_cell_complexity (project:Optional[str]=None, max_classes:int=1,
>                            max_functions:int=2)

*Flag cells with too many definitions (classes/functions).*

nbdev encourages one class or function per cell for: - Granular
testing - Clear cell-level documentation - Literate programming
readability

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_classes</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>1</td>
<td>Maximum classes allowed per cell (default: 1).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_functions</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>2</td>
<td>Maximum functions allowed per cell (default: 2).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result with ‘issues’ list of overly complex
cells.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/tools/lint.py#L908"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### lint_import_order

>  lint_import_order (project:Optional[str]=None)

*Check that `from __future__ import` is in the first export cell.*

Python requires **future** imports to be at the top of a module. In
nbdev, this means the first export cell must contain any **future**
imports, before other export cells.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result with ‘issues’ list of import order
violations.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### lint_dead_exports

>  lint_dead_exports (project:Optional[str]=None,
>                         ignore_patterns:Optional[List[str]]=None)

*Find exported symbols that are never imported elsewhere.*

Scans all notebooks to find exports that no other notebook imports.
These are potential dead code that could be removed.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Project path or alias. Uses current project if not specified.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ignore_patterns</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>List of symbol name patterns to ignore (e.g., [‘main’, ‘cli’]).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Result with ‘dead_exports’ list of unused
symbols.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
